119-hjres-20.one_liner: Cancel a Department of Energy rule setting new efficiency standards for gas water heaters
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule that set new efficiency standards for gas-fired instant water heaters
Blocks efficiency requirements that the Energy Department said would achieve maximum energy savings while being technically possible and economically justified
Prevents the new standards from taking effect after the rule was submitted in December 2024
Stops efficiency rules that would have applied to gas water heaters used in homes and businesses

119-hjres-24.one_liner: Cancel Department of Energy rule setting new energy standards for walk-in coolers and freezers
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule made on December 23, 2024
The rule set new energy conservation standards for walk-in coolers and walk-in freezers
Businesses that make or use walk-in coolers and freezers will not have to follow the new standards
The energy standards were meant to make the equipment use less electricity

119-hjres-25.one_liner: Cancel an IRS rule requiring brokers to report cryptocurrency sales to the government
provisions: Cancels an IRS rule that started December 30, 2024
The rule required brokers who help with cryptocurrency transactions to report sales information to the IRS
This applies to decentralized finance transactions involving digital assets like Bitcoin
Brokers would no longer need to report this information if the bill passes

119-hjres-35.one_liner: Overturn an EPA rule that charges oil and gas companies fees for methane emissions
provisions: Overturns an EPA rule published on November 18, 2024
Stops the EPA from collecting annual fees from oil and gas companies when their methane emissions exceed set waste limits
Removes rules that let companies pay lower fees by offsetting emissions between facilities or qualifying for exemptions
Ends the Methane Emissions Reduction Program's fee collection system

119-hjres-42.one_liner: Cancel a Department of Energy rule changing appliance energy reporting and labeling
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule published on October 9, 2024
The rule changed reporting rules for appliances like washing machines and dishwashers
The rule changed labeling rules for consumer products and industrial equipment
The rule changed enforcement rules for energy efficiency standards
The rule was meant to align reporting with existing energy efficiency rules
The rule was meant to help DOE decide which energy rules apply to which products

119-hjres-60.one_liner: Cancel a National Park Service rule limiting off-road vehicles in Glen Canyon
provisions: Cancels a National Park Service rule issued on January 13, 2025
The rule limited off-road vehicles in Glen Canyon National Recreation Area
The area is in northern Arizona and southeastern Utah
Off-road vehicles would continue under the previous rules

119-hjres-61.one_liner: Cancel an EPA rule limiting air pollution from tire factories
provisions: Cancels an EPA rule about air pollution from tire factories
The EPA rule set limits on air pollution from tire manufacturing
The rule was published in November 2024
The rule required tire factories to control air emissions

119-hjres-75.one_liner: Cancel a Department of Energy rule on commercial refrigerator energy standards
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule from January 21, 2025 that set new energy standards for commercial refrigerators, freezers, and refrigerator-freezers
The rule would have made commercial refrigeration equipment use less electricity while still working properly
Manufacturers would not have to meet the new energy requirements for commercial refrigeration equipment

119-hjres-87.one_liner: Cancel EPA permission for California to set stricter truck emission standards
provisions: Cancels EPA permission from April 2023 that lets California set stricter emission rules for heavy-duty trucks
Removes California's ability to require longer warranties on truck pollution control equipment than federal law requires
Blocks California rules requiring some new trucks to produce zero emissions
Stops California from requiring airport shuttles to be zero-emission vehicles

119-hjres-88.one_liner: Cancel an EPA decision that lets California set its own car emission standards
provisions: Cancels the EPA's January 6, 2025 decision that let California create its own car emission rules
Stops California from implementing its Advanced Clean Cars II regulations
Requires California to follow federal emission standards for cars instead of setting its own

119-hjres-89.one_liner: Block California from requiring stricter pollution controls on heavy trucks than federal rules
provisions: Cancels the EPA's approval that lets California set stricter pollution limits on heavy trucks and buses than federal rules require
Stops new rules that would cut nitrogen oxide pollution from trucks by requiring cleaner engines
Prevents California from enforcing tougher emission standards that truck companies would have to meet when selling vehicles in California
Returns California to following only federal pollution limits for heavy-duty vehicles instead of stricter state standards

119-hr-1.one_liner: Cut taxes for families and businesses while raising the debt limit by $4 trillion
provisions: Makes permanent the individual tax rates of 10%, 12%, 22%, 24%, 32%, 35%, and 37% that were set to expire
Ends government loans that pay student interest and unlimited borrowing loans for graduate students and parents starting July 2026
Requires able-bodied adults ages 18-64 without young children to work 80 hours per month to get SNAP food benefits starting 2029
Requires states to pay 5% to 25% of SNAP food benefit costs based on their error rates starting 2028
Cuts funding for electric vehicle tax credits and many climate programs while adding $100 billion for military spending
Requires adults in Medicaid expansion states to work 80 hours per month starting 2029 to keep their health coverage
Raises the government borrowing limit by $4 trillion to avoid default on federal debt

119-hr-1968.one_liner: Fund the federal government through September 2025
provisions: Funds government programs at 2024 spending levels through September 2025
Prevents a government shutdown when current funding ends March 14, 2025
Extends the National Flood Insurance Program that covers flood damage
Extends Medicare and Medicaid health programs
Extends Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) program that gives cash to families
Extends immigration enforcement programs
Keeps fentanyl-related drugs classified as illegal controlled substances

119-hr-23.one_liner: Punish anyone helping International Criminal Court prosecute Americans and allies
provisions: Punishes foreign persons who help the International Criminal Court prosecute Americans or people from US ally countries
Blocks visas and freezes property of people who assist with court investigations or arrests
Cuts all US funding to the International Criminal Court and stops future payments
Also blocks visas for immediate family members of people who help the court

119-hr-3944.one_liner: Fund military construction and veterans programs for the year starting October 2025
provisions: Funds military construction projects for the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, and reserves
Funds Department of Veterans Affairs programs including health care, benefits, and cemeteries
Funds military family housing construction and maintenance
Funds NATO Security Investment Program
Funds American Battle Monuments Commission and Armed Forces Retirement Home

119-hr-4.one_liner: Cancel $9.4 billion in unspent funding for foreign aid and public broadcasting
provisions: Takes back $9.4 billion that was approved for government agencies but not yet spent
Cancels money for health programs, refugee assistance, and peacekeeping in other countries
Removes funding for programs that support democracy and provide disaster aid worldwide
Cuts money for the Corporation for Public Broadcasting
Stops funding for clean technology programs and development assistance

119-s-1582.one_liner: Create federal rules for digital stablecoins and require companies to back them with real money
provisions: Creates rules for digital coins called stablecoins that companies promise to exchange for a fixed dollar amount
Allows only banks or companies with federal or state permission to issue these coins to Americans
Requires companies to keep real money or similar assets to back each digital coin on a one-to-one basis
Requires companies to publish monthly reports showing what assets back their coins
Lets companies choose federal or state rules, but limits state rules to companies issuing $10 billion or less
Allows foreign companies to sell these coins in America if Treasury approves their home country's rules
Makes companies follow anti-money laundering rules but exempts the coins from securities laws

119-s-2296.one_liner: Set military spending limits and defense policies for 2026
provisions: Sets spending limits for the military for the year starting October 2025 but does not actually give the money
Lets the military buy planes, ships, and missiles
Sets how many active duty and reserve troops the military can have
Approves military construction projects
Requires the Defense Department to create a plan about biotech security threats
Continues a program to strengthen military readiness in the Pacific region through 2026
Removes diversity and inclusion programs from the military including the Chief Diversity Officer job

119-s-331.one_liner: Make all fentanyl-type drugs permanently illegal with set penalties
provisions: Makes all fentanyl-type drugs permanently illegal as the most controlled type of drug
Sets penalties of 10 years minimum in prison for crimes involving 100 grams or more of fentanyl-type drugs
Creates a new way for researchers to get permission to study these banned drugs
Lets researchers use one permit for multiple research locations instead of separate permits
Allows researchers to skip new safety inspections in some cases
Lets approved researchers make small amounts of banned drugs for studies without extra permits

119-s-5.one_liner: Require detention of non-citizens charged with stealing and let states sue over immigration decisions
provisions: Requires the government to detain non-citizens who are charged with, arrested for, or convicted of break-ins, stealing, or shoplifting
Applies to people in the country without permission or who did not have proper documents when entering
Lets states sue the federal government when immigration decisions harm the state by more than $100
States can sue when the government releases someone from detention
States can sue when the government fails to properly check people entering the country or seeking asylum
States can sue when the government fails to detain someone who has been ordered to leave the country

119-s-6.one_liner: Require medical care for children born alive during abortions
provisions: Requires doctors to give the same medical care to babies born alive during abortions as other newborn babies
Requires doctors to send these babies to hospitals right away
Makes doctors report other doctors who don't follow these rules to police
Creates criminal penalties of fines and up to 5 years in prison for doctors who don't comply
Makes intentionally killing a baby born alive during an abortion count as murder
Protects mothers from being charged with crimes under this law
Lets mothers sue doctors who break these rules

119-s-9.one_liner: Ban people born male from women's sports at federally funded schools
provisions: Bans people born male from playing in women's and girls' sports at schools that get federal money
Makes this against Title IX rules that schools must follow
Defines sex based on biology and genetics at birth
Applies to public elementary schools, high schools, and colleges

119-sjres-10.one_liner: Cancel the national emergency about energy declared on January 20, 2025
provisions: Ends the national emergency about energy that started on January 20, 2025
Removes emergency powers that let agencies approve energy projects faster
Stops agencies from using emergency rules to skip normal environmental permits
Ends the Defense Department's emergency authority to build energy infrastructure
Removes the requirement for the Defense Department to assess energy vulnerabilities

119-sjres-11.one_liner: Cancel a rule requiring archaeological surveys for offshore oil and gas drilling
provisions: Cancels a rule requiring oil and gas companies to survey the sea floor for historical items before offshore drilling
The rule made companies look for old objects and human remains that are at least 50 years old
Before this rule, companies only had to do surveys when the government thought historical items might be present
The cancelled rule required these surveys for all offshore drilling projects

119-sjres-12.one_liner: Cancel an EPA rule charging oil and gas companies fees for methane emissions
provisions: Cancels an EPA rule published in November 2024
Stops the government from charging oil and gas companies annual fees for methane emissions
Companies would no longer pay fees when they emit methane above set limits

119-sjres-13.one_liner: Cancel a Treasury rule that ended automatic approvals for some bank mergers
provisions: Cancels a Treasury Department rule published in September 2024 about bank mergers
Restores automatic approval for some bank merger requests under fast review
Brings back shorter application forms for banks wanting to merge
Returns bank merger review process to how it worked before September 2024

119-sjres-18.one_liner: Cancel a rule that would cap overdraft fees at $5 at large banks
provisions: Cancels a Consumer Financial Protection Bureau rule about overdraft fees at large banks
Blocks a rule that would cap overdraft charges at $5 per overdraft
Stops a rule that would let banks charge more than $5 only with written justification
Prevents a rule that would make banks treat overdrafts like credit cards with disclosure requirements

119-sjres-26.one_liner: Block a proposed military sale to Israel
provisions: Stops a proposed military sale to Israel from going through
Blocks the sale of defense articles and services to Israel
Uses Congress's power to disapprove foreign military sales before they happen

119-sjres-28.one_liner: Cancel a rule defining which payment apps get federal oversight
provisions: Cancels a rule that said payment apps with 50 million or more transactions per year must follow federal oversight
Removes the rule that defined which digital payment companies fall under government supervision
Affects payment apps like Venmo, PayPal, and Cash App that handle large numbers of transactions

119-sjres-3.one_liner: Cancel an IRS rule requiring crypto brokers to report digital asset sales
provisions: Cancels an IRS rule issued on December 30, 2024 about reporting crypto transactions
Stops crypto brokers that handle decentralized finance transactions from having to report sales to the IRS
Removes the requirement for these platforms to send information about digital asset sales to tax authorities

119-sjres-31.one_liner: Cancel an EPA air pollution rule from September 2024
provisions: Cancels an EPA rule published September 10, 2024
The rule affects facilities that release toxic air pollutants that stay in the environment and build up in animals and people
These facilities must keep following strict pollution limits even when they reduce emissions enough to qualify for looser limits
Canceling the rule lets facilities switch to looser pollution limits when they cut emissions

119-sjres-33.one_liner: Block a proposed weapons sale to Israel
provisions: Stops a planned sale of weapons and military equipment to Israel
Uses a congressional vote to block the sale from happening
Applies to defense articles and services that were proposed for sale

119-sjres-34.one_liner: Block a proposed military weapons sale to Israel
provisions: Stops the US government from completing one specific planned sale of military equipment to Israel
Prevents weapons and defense services from being transferred to Israel under this particular deal
Blocks only this one sale, not all future military sales to Israel

119-sjres-37.one_liner: End the national emergency that put tariffs on Canadian imports
provisions: Ends the national emergency declared on February 1, 2025
Removes the 25% tariff on most imports from Canada
Removes the 10% tariff on Canadian energy like oil and gas

119-sjres-41.one_liner: Block the sale of certain military weapons to Israel
provisions: Blocks the U.S. from selling certain military weapons to Israel
Stops a weapons sale that the government had planned

119-sjres-49.one_liner: End the national emergency that imposed 10% tariffs on imports
provisions: Ends the national emergency declared by President Trump on April 2, 2025
Stops the 10% tariff on most imports coming into the United States
Removes additional tariffs on imports from certain countries

119-sjres-53.one_liner: Block the sale of weapons and military equipment to Qatar
provisions: Blocks a proposed sale of weapons and military equipment to Qatar
Stops military services from being sold to Qatar
Uses Congress's power to reject weapons sales to other countries

119-sjres-54.one_liner: Block a military weapons sale to the United Arab Emirates
provisions: Stops a planned sale of military weapons and equipment to the United Arab Emirates
Uses Congress's power to disapprove foreign arms sales approved by the executive branch
Prevents the UAE government from receiving the defense items in this proposed deal

119-sjres-55.one_liner: Cancel a federal rule setting safety standards for hydrogen-powered cars
provisions: Cancels a rule from the federal car safety agency about hydrogen-powered vehicles
Removes safety standards for fuel systems in cars that run on hydrogen instead of gasoline
Stops rules about how hydrogen storage containers must be built in these cars

119-sjres-59.one_liner: Stop military action against Iran without congressional approval
provisions: Tells the President to end any current U.S. military action against Iran
Requires Congress to approve any future military action against Iran
Says Congress must either declare war on Iran or pass a specific law allowing military force

119-sjres-7.one_liner: Cancel an FCC rule that allowed schools to buy discounted Wi-Fi hotspots for students
provisions: Cancels an FCC rule from August 2024 about the E-Rate program for schools and libraries
The cancelled rule allowed schools and libraries to buy discounted Wi-Fi hotspots through E-Rate
Students, staff, and library users could take these hotspots home for homework and educational use
After this bill passes, schools and libraries cannot buy these discounted hotspots anymore

119-hconres-14.one_liner: Set federal budget targets for 2025-2034 and order committees to write deficit-changing laws
provisions: Sets budget targets for fiscal years 2025-2034 (October 2024 through September 2034)
Orders 11 House committees to write laws that change the deficit by March 27, 2025
Lets these laws pass the Senate without filibuster using special reconciliation rules
Allows the debt limit to increase by up to $4.5 trillion
Requires committees to find at least $2 trillion in deficit cuts or the debt limit increase gets reduced
Sets spending targets for different government functions like defense and healthcare

119-hconres-30.one_liner: Express support for local law enforcement officers
provisions: States Congress recognizes local law enforcement officers for their work protecting communities
States Congress thanks local law enforcement officers and their families for their service
States Congress honors local law enforcement officers for their sacrifices

119-hjres-104.one_liner: Cancel a federal rule that blocks coal leasing on 1.7 million acres in Montana
provisions: Cancels a Bureau of Land Management rule issued on November 20, 2024
The rule blocked coal leasing on 1.7 million acres of federal land in Montana
Makes those acres available for coal companies to consider for leasing again
Applies to land managed by the Miles City Field Office in Montana

119-hjres-105.one_liner: Cancel a Bureau of Land Management rule limiting oil and gas development in North Dakota
provisions: Cancels a Bureau of Land Management rule issued on January 8, 2025
The rule limited oil and gas drilling in North Dakota areas unlikely to have much oil or gas
The rule limited new coal mining to areas within four miles of existing mines
The rule updated a 1988 plan that guides how the government manages federal land in North Dakota

119-hjres-106.one_liner: Cancel a federal rule on 3.6 million acres in Alaska
provisions: Cancels a Bureau of Land Management rule for Alaska's Central Yukon area
The rule designated 21 environmental areas covering 3.6 million acres
The rule was issued on November 12, 2024

119-hjres-78.one_liner: Cancel a rule that protects longfin smelt fish as endangered
provisions: Cancels a Fish and Wildlife Service rule from July 30, 2024
That rule listed longfin smelt fish in San Francisco Bay as endangered
Removes endangered species protections for this fish
Uses congressional review to overturn the agency rule

119-hr-1039.one_liner: Require VA to review letters sent to veterans and extend loan fees through 2034
provisions: Requires the VA to hire a research center to review letters sent to veterans applying for benefits
VA must report what the review finds and make changes based on the recommendations
Extends current loan fee rates for VA home loans through June 23, 2034

119-hr-1048.one_liner: Require colleges to report smaller foreign gifts and restrict contracts with China and Russia
provisions: Lowers the amount colleges must report for foreign gifts from $250,000 to $50,000
Makes colleges report any gift from China, Russia, or other countries of concern, no matter the amount
Stops colleges from making contracts with China, Russia, or other countries of concern without getting a government waiver first
Makes colleges report if foreign sources control them
Makes college researchers report their foreign gifts and contracts
Makes private colleges with large assets file yearly investment reports
Sets penalties including loss of federal student aid for colleges that break these rules

119-hr-1156.one_liner: Extend time limits for prosecuting fraud in COVID unemployment programs from 5 to 10 years
provisions: Extends time limits from 5 to 10 years for prosecuting people who committed fraud in special unemployment programs created during COVID
Applies to programs that gave unemployment benefits to gig workers, self-employed people, and others who normally cannot get unemployment
Covers both criminal charges and fraud lawsuits against people who filed fake claims
Takes back unused anti-fraud money that was given to the Labor Department in 2021
Does not apply if the 5-year time limit already expired before this bill becomes law

119-hr-1223.one_liner: Require the National Science Foundation to plan cybersecurity upgrades for research ships
provisions: Requires the National Science Foundation to write a plan for upgrading internet and cybersecurity on research ships
Research ships are floating labs where scientists and students study the ocean
The plan must say how much upgrades will cost and how long they will take
The plan must be sent to Congress within 18 months
The National Science Foundation must later report to Congress on how the upgrades are going

119-hr-1228.one_liner: Move the Office of Survivors Assistance to report directly to the VA Secretary
provisions: Moves the Office of Survivors Assistance from the Veterans Benefits Administration to the Office of the Secretary
The office currently operates under the Pension and Fiduciary Service within the Veterans Benefits Administration
After this change, the office will report directly to the VA Secretary instead of through multiple layers
The Office of Survivors Assistance helps families of veterans who have died
This changes how the office is managed within the Department of Veterans Affairs but does not change what services it provides

119-hr-1263.one_liner: Create a working group with Japan, Australia and India for security and economic cooperation
provisions: Tells the State Department to set up a working group with Japan, Australia, and India
Requires the State Department to write a strategy report to Congress about cooperation with these three countries
The report must list current and past joint projects on economic growth, technology, energy, and supply chains
The report must recommend how Congress can help remove barriers to cooperation
The report must recommend what new abilities the U.S. needs to lead joint projects
The report must recommend additional money and resources needed to expand successful projects

119-hr-1286.one_liner: Make the VA get help fixing their veteran claim forms
provisions: The VA must hire a research center to review the forms they send to veterans filing claims
The research center will recommend ways to make the forms clearer and better organized
The VA must make some of these recommended changes within 2 years

119-hr-1326.one_liner: Require the Department of Energy and Department of Agriculture to work together on research projects
provisions: Requires the Department of Energy and Department of Agriculture to work together on research through a written agreement
Creates a contest where federal agencies, national labs, universities, and nonprofits can apply for research funding
Focuses research on machine learning, biofuels, power grid updates, rural technology, and wildfire prevention
Lets the departments share research costs with each other
Supports building research facilities and training workers
Studies ways to make farming work better and reduce greenhouse gas pollution

119-hr-1402.one_liner: Require ticket sellers to show total prices upfront and ban selling tickets they don't have
provisions: Makes ticket sellers show the full ticket price when tickets are first displayed to buyers
Forces sellers to list the base ticket price and each fee separately before completing a purchase
Makes total prices appear in all ads and marketing for tickets
Bans selling tickets that sellers don't actually own or control
Lets secondary sellers offer services to find tickets if they clearly say it's a service, not a ticket
Makes sellers give full refunds when events are canceled or postponed
Gives the Federal Trade Commission power to enforce these rules

119-hr-144.one_liner: Require the Tennessee Valley Authority to report finances and high salaries to Congress
provisions: Brings back a requirement for the Tennessee Valley Authority to send annual financial reports to Congress each March
Changes which employee salaries must be reported from those earning more than $1,500 per year to those earning more than about $140,000 per year
Requires reporting the names, salaries, and job duties of high-earning employees

119-hr-1442.one_liner: Ban consumer products containing 10% or more sodium nitrite
provisions: Makes consumer products with 10% or more sodium nitrite banned hazardous products
Does not apply to drugs and medical devices
Does not apply to cosmetics and food products

119-hr-1491.one_liner: Make disaster deadline delays count as tax extensions
provisions: Makes disaster deadline delays count the same as regular deadline extensions for tax refunds
Changes how the IRS calculates tax refund amounts when disasters postpone filing deadlines
Includes disaster delays when the IRS figures out how much to refund taxpayers
Changes when the IRS can send tax payment notices during disasters

119-hr-1503.one_liner: Block property and visas of people who harvest organs by force
provisions: Requires the President to make a list of people and groups who take organs by force or help traffic people for organ harvesting
Blocks those people from getting US visas and freezes any property they own in America
Lets the State Department deny or take away passports from Americans convicted of selling human organs for money
Only applies to passport denial if the person is in prison or on probation and used a passport or crossed a border during the crime

119-hr-152.one_liner: Require FEMA to make disaster aid applications easier and study damage assessments
provisions: Requires FEMA to study how to make disaster aid applications less time-consuming and less repetitive for people applying for help
Requires FEMA to create a plan to regularly track and report how much federal disaster aid money gets awarded
Requires FEMA to form a working group that meets regularly to find duplicate work in damage assessments after disasters
Requires the working group to study whether one federal agency should do all damage assessments instead of multiple agencies
Requires the working group to study using drones and other new technology to speed up damage assessments
Requires FEMA to report its findings to Congress within two years and post the report on FEMA's website for the public to read

119-hr-1526.one_liner: Limit federal court orders that block government rules nationwide
provisions: Federal district courts can currently issue orders blocking government rules nationwide
This bill would require court orders to only affect the specific people in the lawsuit
Courts could no longer stop government policies nationwide from a single case

119-hr-153.one_liner: Require federal agencies to publish quarterly reports on disaster assistance spending
provisions: Requires federal agencies that give disaster assistance to publish reports every three months
Reports must show the total amount of money the agency gave out for disasters
Reports must show how much of that money was actually spent
Reports must list all projects and activities that used the disaster money
Makes this information available to the public online
Applies to the Small Business Administration, Housing and Urban Development, and other agencies that provide disaster help

119-hr-1534.one_liner: Fund research into cement, concrete, and asphalt that produce less pollution
provisions: Creates a 7-year research program at the Department of Energy
Program funds projects to make cement, concrete, and asphalt that release less pollution
Department of Energy picks government agencies, nonprofits, schools, and companies for test projects
Projects focus on capturing pollution and using less energy when making these materials
Department can stop funding projects early if materials that create less pollution become available at normal prices
Program ends 7 years after the bill becomes law

119-hr-164.one_liner: Let electric companies do disaster prevention work when getting federal emergency help to restore power
provisions: Lets electric companies use federal emergency money to both fix power outages and strengthen power lines against future storms
Allows electric companies to get FEMA money to protect the same power facilities that are getting emergency repair help

119-hr-1642.one_liner: Require business centers to tell small businesses about hiring career school graduates
provisions: Requires Small Business Development Centers and Women's Business Centers to tell small businesses about hiring career school graduates
Lets these centers connect small businesses with career schools to help students and graduates find jobs

119-hr-165.one_liner: Place 40 acres in South Dakota into special tribal ownership for two Sioux tribes
provisions: Places 40 acres in Oglala Lakota County, South Dakota into special tribal ownership for the Oglala Sioux Tribe and Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe
Under this ownership, the tribes own the land but cannot sell it or borrow money against it without federal approval
Requires the land to be maintained as a memorial and sacred site
Blocks any commercial development or casinos on the land
This bill requires a study of cybersecurity risks in mobile phone networks.
The study must examine threats to the networks that carry cell phone calls, texts, and data.
Recommendations must be provided to Congress on how to improve mobile network security.
119-hr-1709.one_liner: This bill requires a study of cybersecurity risks in mobile phone networks.

119-hr-1717.one_liner: Give legal power to a council advising FCC on network safety
provisions: Creates legal authority for a council that advises the FCC on communications network security and reliability
Requires council members from communications companies, public interest groups, and federal, state, tribal, and local governments
Bars council membership for representatives of companies controlled by foreign adversaries or that pose national security threats
Removes the normal 2-year limit that ends government advisory groups, letting this council continue indefinitely

119-hr-1770.one_liner: Create a pilot program using artificial intelligence to help track unsafe consumer products
provisions: Creates a pilot program for the Consumer Product Safety Commission to use artificial intelligence
The commission can use AI to track injury trends from consumer products
The commission can use AI to find dangerous products
The commission can use AI to watch for recalled products still being sold
The commission can use AI to find imported products that don't meet safety rules
Makes the Commerce Department write a report about using blockchain technology for consumer protection
Makes the Federal Trade Commission write a report about stopping unfair practices with digital tokens

119-hr-1804.one_liner: Require yearly reports on agents who help with small business loans
provisions: Requires the Small Business Administration to publish a yearly report about agents who help people apply for government-backed small business loans
The report must count how many agents work with loan applicants and what types of services they provide
The report must count fraudulent loans that involved these agents
The report must track loans where agents helped with applications
The report must show the total dollar amount paid to agents in referral fees

119-hr-186.one_liner: Require the Medal of Honor monument to be built in the main National Mall area
provisions: Requires an approved monument honoring Medal of Honor recipients to be built in the main National Mall area
The main National Mall area runs from the Capitol to the Lincoln Memorial and from the White House to the Jefferson Memorial

119-hr-187.one_liner: Create online maps showing where people can fish and boat on federal waters
provisions: Requires the Forest Service and Interior Department to create shared rules for collecting information about rivers and lakes on federal land
Agencies must put maps online showing where people can fish, boat, and access waterways within 5 years
Agencies must update waterway access information at least twice each year
Agencies must update fishing rules online right away when changes happen
Creates a way for people to ask questions or give feedback about the waterway information

119-hr-1917.one_liner: Make official a federal program that marks hatchery fish in the Great Lakes
provisions: Makes official a federal program that marks hatchery fish in the Great Lakes
The program has been running since 2010 but did not have legal authority
Marks help show which fish came from hatcheries and which are wild
Lets the Fish and Wildlife Service buy equipment for marking more fish
Lets the agency hire more workers for the program
Requires sharing fish data with state and tribal fish managers

119-hr-1919.one_liner: Stop the Federal Reserve from creating a digital dollar
provisions: Stops Federal Reserve banks from offering banking services directly to people
Stops the Federal Reserve from holding bank accounts for individuals
Stops the Federal Reserve from creating a digital dollar
Stops the Federal Reserve from using digital currency to set interest rates
Stops the Federal Reserve from testing or studying digital currency, with some exceptions allowed

119-hr-192.one_liner: Require Amtrak to publicly report executive pay and bonus amounts
provisions: Amtrak must report the base pay of each executive in its yearly report to Congress
Amtrak must report all bonus money paid to executives
The report must explain how bonuses are decided and what goals executives must meet
Amtrak must post this report on its public website

119-hr-1998.one_liner: Block visas and freeze property of people who commit piracy
provisions: The President must stop pirates from getting visas to enter the US
The President must freeze the money and property that pirates own in the US
People who break these new rules can be fined or sent to jail
These rules apply to foreign individuals and groups that commit piracy

119-hr-2035.one_liner: Require all Department of Transportation cargo to be shipped on U.S.-flagged vessels
provisions: Changes the rule from 50% to 100% of federal cargo that must be shipped on U.S.-flagged vessels
Applies to all cargo the Department of Transportation buys or finances
Covers cargo DOT contracts for itself and cargo it pays others to ship
Ships must be available at fair and reasonable prices
Must allow fair participation of U.S.-flagged ships across different regions

119-hr-2056.one_liner: Require Washington D.C. to cooperate with federal immigration enforcement
provisions: Stops Washington D.C. from limiting cooperation with federal immigration enforcement
Requires D.C. to share information about people's citizenship and immigration status with federal agencies
